name: SharpSploit
summary: [SharpSploit](https://github.com/cobbr/SharpSploit) is a .NET post-exploitation library written in C# that aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET and make the use of offensive .NET easier for red teamers.

documentation.readme: SharpSploit is a .NET post-exploitation library written in C# that aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET and make the use of offensive .NET easier for red teamers.
usage_sections: Justin Bui (@youslydawg) - For contributing the SharpSploit.Enumeration.Host.CreateProcessDump() function.
Matt Graeber (@mattifestation), Will Schroeder (@harmj0y), and Ruben (@FuzzySec) - For their work on PowerSploit.
Will Schroeder (@harmj0y) - For the PowerView project.
Alexander Leary (@0xbadjuju) - For the Tokenvator project.
James Foreshaw (@tiraniddo) - For his discovery of the token duplication UAC bypass technique documented here.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `SharpSploit` from its upstream repository, then run `SharpSploit --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. SharpSploit is a .NET post-exploitation library written in C# that aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET and make the use of offensive .NET easier for red teamers.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_windows, ad] in the Windows / AD category. Fits when the engagement needs: [SharpSploit](https://github.com/cobbr/SharpSploit) is a .NET post-exploitation library written in C# that aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET and make the use of offensive .NET easier for red teamers.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: SharpSploit is a .NET post-exploitation library written in C# that aims to highlight the attack surface of .NET and make the use of offensive .NET easier for red teamers.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/nishang (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/mimikatz (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/lsassy (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
